findings.evidence: `Weight.lean:88` has `def chi (k : V → ℤ) (_hk : P.IsCharacteristicVector k)`, while `Weight.lean:90` is just `-P.chiNumerator k x / 2`; the nearby existing API `Characteristic.lean:65` already defines `characteristicVectors := { k : V → ℤ // P.IsCharacteristicVector k }`.
findings.fix: Either define the raw arithmetic `chi (k) (x) := -P.chiNumerator k x / 2` without `hk` and keep characteristicness only on lemmas like `two_mul_chi`, or define the domain as the existing subtype `P.characteristicVectors` so the assumption is part of the input rather than an unused proof argument.
findings.issue: `chi` requires `_hk : P.IsCharacteristicVector k` but the body ignores it, making the definition artificially proof-parameterized even though the arithmetic expression is defined for every `k`.

summary: The new weight API carries a visibly unused characteristic proof in the main definition, so the definition is not at the natural level for this layer.
